edges.description: Reduced enzyme activity allows neuronal GM1 ganglioside accumulation.
edges.source: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

edges.target: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

meta.association: Biallelic GLB1 variants with partial residual beta-galactosidase activity

evidence.snippet: GM1 gangliosidosis is a progressive, neurosomatic, lysosomal storage disorder caused by mutations in the GLB1 gene encoding the enzyme β-galactosidase.

nodes.description: GM1 ganglioside accumulates in neuronal lysosomes, impairing cell physiology and precipitating progressive neurodegeneration at a slower pace than the infantile form.

evidence.snippet: Absent or reduced β-galactosidase activity leads to the accumulation of β-linked galactose-containing glycoconjugates including the glycosphingolipid (GSL) GM1-ganglioside in neuronal tissue.

nodes.description: Biallelic GLB1 variants reduce lysosomal beta-galactosidase activity. Residual activity higher than in the infantile form yields a later-onset, more slowly progressive neuronal GM1 ganglioside accumulation.
nodes.id: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

evidence.snippet: Mutation of the GLB1 gene, which codes for β-gal, prevents cleavage of the terminal β-1,4-linked galactose residue from GM1 ganglioside.
